It looks like the change to the disk format for the changelog users is not being handled properly.  It may be easier to just re-use the cur_padding field to store a 32-bit time and use something similar to time_after() to compare times (to handle the Y2038 overflow issue), since it isn't useful to have changelog records more than 2^32s = 68 years old.

Patch Set 12:

Note that I landed a patch to the upstream kernel which added a time_after32() helper macro:

/**
+ * time_after32 - compare two 32-bit relative times
+ * @a: the time which may be after @b
+ * @b: the time which may be before @a
+ *
+ * time_after32(a, b) returns true if the time @a is after time @b.
+ * time_before32(b, a) returns true if the time @b is before time @a.
+ *
+ * Similar to time_after(), compare two 32-bit timestamps for relative
+ * times.  This is useful for comparing 32-bit seconds values that can't
+ * be converted to 64-bit values (e.g. due to disk format or wire protocol
+ * issues) when it is known that the times are less than 68 years apart.
+ */
+#define time_after32(a, b)     ((s32)((u32)(b) - (u32)(a)) < 0)
+#define time_before32(b, a)    time_after32(a, b)

so this could be added to lustre_compat.h under "#ifndef time_after32"
to be used here.

It would be good to get this patch included into 2.11, and also 2.10.x
since users are still hitting this problem.

Patch Set 12:

(4 comments)

Andreas, I had not a lot of time to think about it but, if I understand (and agree for all the good reasons you have raised and that I had delayed to first work on my patch functionality) you and Henri really prefer cur_padding field usage instead of a new/additional field, what I am still unsure is which time unit has to be stored there...
To be honest, I am not sure to fully understand how these time_...() macros can solve the Y2038 wrap (to negative value?) of signed 32-bits time_t counters. So, is it expected that a function like get_seconds() will start to return negative values at this time ? If yes, I understand that your macros will help if maximum allowed difference is less than 68 years but this only until Y2106, right ?
Also, I am asking myself if this cur_padding 32-bits field could not better be used to store a value in hours or days (since epoch) to avoid this limitations when working with seconds ??

commit.subject: LU-7340 mdd: changelogs garbage collection
commit.message:
LU-7340 mdd: changelogs garbage collection

When changelogs are almost full (few number of
free entries in catalog), try to recover some space
by unregistering users that are idle since too long,
based on new Changelog User record field (in fact,
using previous cur_padding unused field) to keep
track of last user's changelog cancel request time,
or based on gap between user index and current
ChangeLog record, for older registered user.
sanity/test_160[f,g] have been added to verify feature.
